tools.name: web_search_exa
tools.description:
Search the web for any topic and get clean, ready-to-use content.

      Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, people, companies, or answering questions about any topic.
      Returns: Clean text content from top search results.

      Query tips: 
      describe the ideal page, not keywords. "blog post comparing React and Vue performance" not "React vs Vue".
      Use category:people / category:company to search through Linkedin profiles / companies respectively.
      If highlights are insufficient, follow up with web_fetch_exa on the best URLs.

query.description: Natural language search query. Should be a semantically rich description of the ideal page, not just keywords. Optionally include category:<type> (company, people) to focus results — e.g. 'category:people John Doe software engineer'.

numResults.description: Number of search results to return (must be a number, default: 10).

tools.name: web_search_advanced_exa
tools.description:
Advanced web search with full control over filters, domains, dates, and content options.

Best for: When you need specific filters like date ranges, domain restrictions, or category filters.
Not recommended for: Simple searches - use web_search_exa instead.
Returns: Search results with optional highlights, summaries, and subpage content.

query.description: Search query - can be a question, statement, or keywords

numResults.description: Number of results (must be a number, 1-100, default: 10)

type.description: Search type - 'auto': high quality and works with all filters (recommended), 'fast': quick results, 'instant': fastest results

category.description: Filter results to a specific category

includeDomains.description: Only include results from these domains (e.g., ['arxiv.org', 'github.com'])

startPublishedDate.description: Only include results published after this date (ISO 8601: YYYY-MM-DD)

endPublishedDate.description: Only include results published before this date (ISO 8601: YYYY-MM-DD)

startCrawlDate.description: Only include results crawled after this date (ISO 8601: YYYY-MM-DD)

endCrawlDate.description: Only include results crawled before this date (ISO 8601: YYYY-MM-DD)

includeText.description: Only include results containing ALL of these text strings

excludeText.description: Exclude results containing ANY of these text strings

userLocation.description: ISO country code for geo-targeted results (e.g., 'US', 'GB', 'DE')

additionalQueries.description: Additional query variations to expand search coverage

textMaxCharacters.description: Max characters for text extraction per result (must be a positive number)

contextMaxCharacters.description: Max characters for context string (must be a positive number, not included by default)

highlightsMaxCharacters.description: Maximum total characters across all highlights per URL (must be a number). Preferred over highlightsNumSentences.

highlightsNumSentences.description: Deprecated: mapped to ~1333 chars/sentence. Use highlightsMaxCharacters instead.

highlightsPerUrl.description: Deprecated: currently ignored server-side. Use highlightsMaxCharacters instead.

maxAgeHours.description: Maximum age of cached content in hours. 0 = always fetch fresh content, omit = use cached content with fresh fetch fallback (must be a number)

livecrawlTimeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for fetching fresh content when maxAgeHours triggers a live fetch (must be a number)

subpages.description: Number of subpages to crawl from each result (must be a number, 1-10)

subpageTarget.description: Keywords to target when selecting subpages

tools.name: web_fetch_exa
tools.description:
Read a webpage's full content as clean markdown. Use after web_search_exa when highlights are insufficient or to read any URL.

Best for: Extracting full content from known URLs. Batch multiple URLs in one call.
Returns: Clean text content and metadata from the page(s).

urls.description: URLs to read. Batch multiple URLs in one call.

maxCharacters.description: Maximum characters to extract per page (must be a positive number, default: 3000)
